With less than two months until the resolution date (1 July 2025), there is no credible evidence suggesting Vladimir Putin is about to leave office voluntarily, be overthrown, or die. He remains firmly in control, having recently ordered a massive military conscription (160,000 men in April 2025) and declared strategic ceasefires. Furthermore, rumors regarding his poor health have circulated for years without materializing, and an April 2025 retrospective noted these rumors have been 'greatly exaggerated'. The base rate of an entrenched autocratic leader dying or being ousted in any given 50-day window is exceptionally low, solidly aligning with the crowd forecast of 1% from May 1st. Given the lack of any breaking developments to the contrary, the probability is set to the minimum allowed bound (0.02).

The question explicitly asks if South Korea's TFR will increase from 2023 to 2025 (i.e., be strictly > 0.72 in 2025). The most recent official preliminary data from early 2025 reveals that the TFR for 2024 already surged to 0.75, breaking a 9-year negative streak. Furthermore, the number of marriages in 2024 leaped by a massive 14.9% – the fastest pace on record – building on a post-pandemic recovery that began in 2023. Because nearly all births in South Korea occur within wedlock, a surge in marriages overwhelmingly predicts a sustained or further-accelerating increase in births over the following 1-2 years. Therefore, the momentum from the late-2024 marriage and birth acceleration heavily buffers 2025 demographics, significantly insulating the birth rate from dropping back down below the 0.72 threshold even in scenarios where short-term political instability (like the late-2024 martial law incident) introduces some caution among couples. Given the strong 2024 baseline of 0.75 and the powerful forward indicator of the 15% marriage spike, the probability of the 2025 TFR remaining above 0.72 is extremely high.

The criteria strictly require two consecutive negative quarters in 2025 based on the advance estimates. Given the BEA's April 30 advance estimate for Q1 2025 was -0.3%, the first requirement for consecutive negative quarters is live. If Q2 is negative, the question resolves YES. However, GDPNow currently projects +2.3% for Q2, and the negative Q1 print seems partially driven by trade anomalies (e.g., gold exports/imports), which often leads to a snap-back in the following quarter. Assuming Q2 is highly likely to be positive, the question would then heavily depend on a new recession forming explicitly in Q3 AND Q4. While a recent Reuters poll from April 17 places a 45% chance of a US recession in the next 12 months due to tariff concerns and Fed rate decisions, the fact that a Q4 2025/Q1 2026 recession resolves to NO significantly compresses the available timeframe. This makes a YES resolution reliant either on an unexpected Q2 negative print, or a perfectly timed Q3/Q4 contraction sequence. Balancing the locked-in negative Q1 and the high overall recession risk against the timing constraints and likely positive Q2 bounce, a ~43% probability is optimal.

The US economy grew steadily throughout 2023 and 2024, with solid GDP expansion across all quarters (e.g., 2.3% annualized in Q4 2024). A recession is defined by the NBER Business Cycle Dating Committee as a significant decline in economic activity lasting more than a few months. Given the sustained growth in real GDP, employment, and income through 2023 and 2024, it is virtually impossible that the NBER will retroactively declare a recession for those years by the end of 2025. The market consensus as of May 1, 2025 matches this, pricing the outcome at around 1%. Therefore, the probability is set to the floor of 2%.

Historical data suggests that years experiencing a >5% daily drop in the S&P 500 have roughly a 40-50% chance of experiencing another one (volatility clustering, seen in 1987, 2008, 2020, but averted in 1997, 1998, 2011). 
On April 4, 2025, the S&P 500 dropped 6% amidst aggressive tariff announcements (search_0_result_0) triggering a surge in the VIX up to 60. By May 1, 2025, the Manifold crowd forecast stood at 0.47 (47%). 
Since early April, however, the market has undergone a significant "V-shaped" recovery. Reports on May 4-5 indicate the market went on its longest winning streak since 2004, fully recouping the "Liberation Day" tariff losses (search_4_result_4). The VIX retreated significantly, likely falling to the 20s or low 30s. The absence of a second major drop between April 5 and May 11, combined with the subsiding immediate panic, shaves a few percentage points off the May 1st crowd estimate through time decay and returning calmness. 
Nevertheless, the root cause (a disruptive trade war and recession fears) leaves the market vulnerable to further unpredictable policy shocks later in 2025. Therefore, a secondary >5% daily drop remains highly plausible. I will estimate slightly below the recent 47% market anchor to account for the current rapid recovery trend and time decay.

The market value on May 1 was 34%. Carson the Terrier is arguably the most accomplished small dog surfer of recent years, finishing 3rd in Top Dog in 2022 and 1st in 2023. However, he fell out of the Top 3 in 2024 (though he placed 2nd in the Small Dog division). One key downside is his age: articles from 2024 note he was an 8-year veteran of the sport and 10 years old, meaning he will be 11 for the 2025 competition. This advances dog aging risks, which likely explains his slight dip in performance against younger or larger dogs (like the 2024 winner, Cacau the Labrador). Given his age and the rise of international competitors, combined with the fact that he missed the podium last year, I estimate his chances of bouncing back into the overall Top 3 to be slightly lower than his peak years, though still substantial given his divisional dominance. I'm adjusting slightly downward from the recent market price of 34% to 32% to account for aging and increased difficulty.

The Manifold community placed the probability at 46% on May 1, 2025. Early May data shows that TSLA was trading between $285 and $287. Since the market price was 46%, it is clear that TSLA has not dropped below $200 between January 1, 2025 and May 1, 2025. Tesla is a highly volatile stock, and a drop from $285 to $199 (about a 30% drop) within the remaining 8 months of the year is entirely plausible—aligning effectively with the ~46% option-implied probability. Because only about 10 days have elapsed since the May 1 baseline and there has been no dramatic movement to the upside or downside beyond recent levels, the underlying probability remains largely unchanged. I estimate it slightly lower than the May 1 baseline to account for time decay.

The market correctly prices this at ~90%. There is overwhelming evidence that NEOM / The Line has been scaled back, mostly notably the April 2024 reports that the 170km plan has been reduced to just 2.4km by 2030, and the March 2025 internal audits confirming a $9 trillion price tag and a 50+ year timeline. High-level executives have fled, and multiple elements of NEOM (like the $1.5B desalination plant) have been explicitly cancelled. The resolution criteria mentions "Scaled back to something vastly more modest (at my disgression)" and the creator notes they believe the project is unrealistic. A reduction from 170km to 2.4km unquestionably meets the definition of 'vastly more modest', and with the creator's stated skepticism, a YES resolution is highly probable. The only reason it's not 99% is the slight possibility that the creator accepts the Saudi government's excuse that 2.4km is just 'Phase 1' and not an official scale-back of the final vision, but given the creator's discretion and the market at 90%, 92% is a solid estimate.

Elon Musk made a highly publicized $97.4 billion hostle bid for OpenAI in February 2025, which expired on May 10, 2025. OpenAI's board unanimously rejected the offer, publicly characterizing it as a "sham bid" intended to disrupt their fundraising in an April 2025 countersuit. Additionally, recent reporting from early May 2025 confirms that the bitter legal feud between Musk and OpenAI continues unabated. The resolution criteria require a 'formal-ish announcement from both parties' agreeing to start the acquisition process. Given the existing hostility, ongoing lawsuits, antitrust hurdles, and Microsoft's strong ties to OpenAI, the probability of both parties agreeing to a Musk takeover before the August 31, 2025 deadline is negligibly small. A 0.02 probability heavily factors in these realities while leaving a tiny tail-risk margin for black swan events (e.g., an unprecedented legal settlement resulting in a buyout).

The forecast window to achieve and publicly announce AGI is only ~7.5 months (before Jan 1, 2026). While there is definitely massive hype around advanced capabilities in 2025 (with OpenAI’s o3 model passing ARC-AGI and initially some claims from Sam Altman about 2025), Altman has since clarified in a Jan 2025 interview that his previous 2025 target was just a "random number" set 10 years ago. Other leading labs definitively place their timelines further out — Anthropic expects major leaps around 2026-2027 but refuses to use the "AGI" label, and DeepMind’s leadership (Demis Hassabis) and recent 145-page paper explicitly forecast true AGI closer to 2030. Furthermore, OpenAI is severely legally and financially disincentivized from officially "announcing" AGI right now, as recent late 2024 reports show their Microsoft contract binds them to a newly formed financial definition of AGI requiring the system to generate at least $100 billion in profits for its earliest investors—a milestone they are currently no where near. With the major labs actively pushing realistic timelines to 2027-2030+ and severe structural hurdles to any early formal announcement, the likelihood of a legitimate, declared AGI before 2026 is extremely low. I estimate 2% (the floor).

As of mid-May 2025, reports from March have confirmed that talks between the NFL and ESPN for an equity stake have not only been resurrected but are 'gaining steam' and nearing a completion for a massive asset swap (NFL Media assets for an equity stake in ESPN). This ties in cleanly with ESPN's strategic imperative to heavily bolster their content offerings prior to the launch of their standalone direct-to-consumer (DTC) flagship offering expected in Fall 2025. The lengthy negotiation timeline (having initially started before January 2024, dying off in late 2024, and resurrecting in early 2025) suggests original hurdles have likely been addressed. The Manifold market consensus on May 1 was 0.69, reflecting high optimism drawn from these late March 2025 developments. Maintaining the estimate at 0.69 balances the strong fundamental incentives for both parties against the inherent risks and red tape of finalizing a $2B partnership between media/sports giants by the end of the year.

The initial proposal to ban the Tush Push only garnered 16 of the necessary 24 votes at the Annual League Meeting in early April. However, rather than failing, it was tabled. The NFL Competition Committee and Commissioner Goodell have since reframed the rule from a specific ban targeting one team's formation to a broader safety rule prohibiting all pushing and pulling of a runner. Goodell explicitly stated on April 25 that he expects owners to "reach a consensus" at the Spring League Meeting on May 20-21. A May 5 report by the Washington Post confirms this expansive version is expected to have "strong support." Given the reframing of the rule to address philosophical objections and the Commissioner's public confidence, there is a strong chance they secure the remaining 8 votes. The May 20-21 meeting aligns perfectly with the May 22 resolution date. Because changing 8 votes relies on the successful reframing of the rule and the owners accepting the "injury risk" premise, it's not a certainty, leading me to forecast a 60% probability.

The market stood at ~0.31 on May 1, 2025. Recent news reveals that despite a potential trigger for a feud—Musk publicly opposing Trump's core tariff policy and calling Trump trade adviser Peter Navarro 'dumber than a sack of bricks' in April 2025—Trump showed unusual restraint. Instead of attacking Musk, Trump defended him against protesters ('hasn't been treated properly'), praised his work ('doing a fantastic job'), and framed his upcoming departure from the administration as a natural return to his businesses, though Trump did casually assert he 'doesn't need him.' This indicates Trump holds Musk in a special category and has a much higher threshold for disparaging him compared to typical cabinet members. Because the most obvious point of friction has passed without an explicit insult, and an amicable exit narrative is being constructed, the probability of Trump issuing a public insult/belittling nickname before November 2025 is relatively low, aligning closely with the market's ~31% assessment.

No perfect March Madness brackets survived the first weekend of the 2025 tournament, as extensively tracked and reported by multiple outlets. As per the rules of the X/Uber Eats bracket challenge, the prize for an imperfect but best bracket is $100,000, not the trip to Mars, and the resolution criteria explicitly state that winning the $100,000 without a perfect bracket won't count. Given that achieving a perfect bracket has never been verified in history and all brackets this year have visibly busted, it is near impossible for X to truthfully declare that someone won a trip to Mars on the basis of a perfect bracket. Therefore, the chance of this resolving affirmative is at the floor of acceptable probability inputs.

The market was at 64% on May 1st. First-time game development projects have a high failure rate due to underestimating the final polish and balancing phases, especially for a game with 10-30 levels. However, the use of Godot for a 2D tower defense game is highly feasible, and having a dedicated artist (@TobiasVyseri) who has already completed many assets removes a major bottleneck. The project is a satiric callback to Flash games, suggesting a focused, modest scope. The update on March 6 added a "minimum quality standard," reducing the chance of a cheap technicality win, which might slightly depress the true probability of success. The developer's self-admitted slow start in programming (taking days to position a rectangle) highlights early learning curve friction, but a full year (until Dec 31, 2025) is typically sufficient to overcome this for a basic tower defense game. I am heavily anchoring on the 64% market estimate but adjusting slightly downward to 62% because first-time devs almost universally overrun their schedules, and the strict quality/reception rule adds a layer of difficulty.

In 2024, DeepMind's AlphaProof and AlphaGeometry roughly achieved a Silver medal level (28 points, 1 point shy of Gold) on the IMO, although it took up to three days for some problems. Since then, reasoning models have rapidly advanced. OpenAI's o3 released in late 2024 / early 2025 achieved a Gold medal at the IOI (programming) under strict human time limits, demonstrating that test-time compute can be heavily optimized for speed. Furthermore, the 2025 resolution criteria enforce a 4.5-hour wall-clock limit per set of 3 problems, but place **no limits on the computational resources used during that time**. This severely favors AI labs like Google DeepMind and OpenAI, who can parallelize proof search across massive GPU clusters, rendering the 4.5-hour constraint much less restrictive than it appears. With rapid improvements in o-series models and AlphaGeometry 2 (which now averages 19 seconds per geometry proof), plus the massive prestige incentive for these companies to try, the probability of successfully clearing the Gold threshold (top ~50 humans) by August 2025 is very high. I estimate a 73% chance of resolution.

The base rate derived from recent betting odds (e.g., +255 to +260, implying a ~24% vig-free true probability) captures the Celtics' current precarious but capable position perfectly. After shockingly losing Games 1 and 2 to the Knicks at home (blowing 20+ point leads in both), the Celtics are currently trailing 1-2 in the Eastern Conference Semifinals. However, an emphatic 115-93 away victory in Game 3 demonstrates their underlying strength. The market showed immense respect for Boston by pricing them as a near-coinflip (-105) to win the series even when down 0-2. Navigating the rest of the playoffs remains difficult: I estimate they have roughly a 55-60% chance to overcome the 1-2 deficit against New York, about a 65% chance to win the Eastern Conference if they advance, and a 50-60% chance in the NBA Finals against a heavy Western Conference favorite like the Thunder or Nuggets. Multiplying these out yields ~18-23%. With some minor lingering injury concerns (Brown's knee, Tatum's tendinopathy), but strong metrics supporting a comeback, a 23% probability effectively synthesis the sportsbook consensus and situational analysis at this exact point in time (May 11).

Beyond Meat's primary threat to survival is its $1.1B zero-interest convertible note, but this debt does not mature until March 15, 2027. The company's cash runway was previously a major concern (causing the high community prediction), but on May 7, 2025, they announced raising an additional $100 million in debt financing from a non-profit plant-based advocate. Adding this to their reported $115.8 million cash on hand at the end of Q1 2025, their liquidity exceeds $215 million. With projected annual cash burn for 2025 estimated at less than $85 million, they have far more than enough runway to clear the end of 2025. Furthermore, multiple experts and analysts have stated that the 'base case' for a Chapter 11 filing is now 2027 when the notes mature. Since the resolution date requires bankruptcy before January 1, 2026, the short-term triggers have been effectively neutralized. The only remaining risks are sudden operational collapse or unexpected technical defaults, keeping the probability at a very low 6%.

While the Metaculus community estimate of 57% on May 1, 2025, suggests strong sentiment that a federal AI security bill (possibly targeting Chinese espionage of frontier models) could pass by the end of 2025, searches have mostly returned voluntary initiatives (like the Secure A.I. Act) or state-level bills (like California's SB 1047 or Illinois HB 3506c). Historically, passing mandatory regulations for the tech industry is difficult and slow in Congress. Under a Republican-led government in 2025, there may be pushback against sweeping regulations, although national security concerns regarding frontier AI models could drive bipartisan support to attach cybersecurity mandates to a must-pass bill like the NDAA. Given the strict resolution criteria—a federal law passed by Dec 31, 2025, that mandates *all* U.S. actors possessing powerful models to take security measures—my probability is somewhat lower than the community consensus at 44%, reflecting the lack of a prominent, explicitly mandatory bill currently advancing.

As of mid-May 2025, the major AI copyright and training data lawsuits (including NYT v. OpenAI, UMG v. Anthropic, Doe v. GitHub, and various class actions) are either bogged down in protracted discovery disputes, dealing with interlocutory appeals, or have just recently survived motions to dismiss. None have a trial date set that could reasonably lead to a final judgment by December 31, 2025. Complex, high-stakes US litigation typically takes several years to reach a trial, let alone a final enforceable order. Furthermore, the companies have existential incentives to fight these claims on "fair use" grounds rather than accepting a swift $>100M judgment. While a massive early settlement is theoretically possible, courts would have to approve it as a formal order, and the timeline for class-action settlement approval procedures alone usually spans 6-12 months. Given the absence of any newly announced settlements or massive FTC/DOJ fines under the current administration, a $>100M court order before the end of 2025 is exceedingly unlikely.

The probability is firmly pegged at the minimum allowed by the platform rules (typically 1% or 2% for almost impossible events depending on calibration). 

Ukraine heavily relies on external financial, economic, and military support from the US and European allies. All of these allies strictly support the Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty (NPT). Developing an indigenous nuclear weapons program would certainly result in the total cessation of Western support, placing Ukraine's survival in critical peril. This makes a real Ukrainian nuclear program virtually unthinkable as a matter of policy.

A rumor occasionally emerges, including one allegedly referenced in an internal Ukrainian paper, but official sources vehemently deny this, and any actual development is assessed to take roughly 5 years, let alone the diplomatic fallout.

Furthermore, a search of major news outlets (Reuters, NYT, WaPo) up to May 2025 yielded absolutely no credible reports of Ukraine launching or running a nuclear weapons program. The Metaculus market prediction as of early May 2025 was 1% (0.01). 

Since there is zero evidence of an official program, very high structural disincentives, and consistent official denials, I forecast 2% (0.02).

As of May 11, 2025, there has been no reported human-to-human (H2H) transmission of H5N1 locally or globally by the CDC. The US outbreak linked to dairy cattle has caused ~70 human cases so far, but robust surveillance shows all cases have been animal-to-human. Furthermore, the most prominent suspected case of H2H spread (the Missouri cluster in late 2024) was definitively ruled out by CDC serological testing. Sequencing of recent human strains confirms the virus retains strong avian-receptor binding without the key mutations needed for efficient mammalian transmission. While historically there are rare instances of limited, non-sustained H2H H5N1 transmission (e.g. within families), none have occurred during the current intense outbreak despite ample opportunities. With only 7.5 months remaining in the resolution window, the window for a H2H event to occur and be officially reported by the CDC is narrowing. I am forecasting 7%, half of the early May community prediction of 14% (which was likely elevated due to uncertainty over unconfirmed clusters that have since resolved negative).

As of May 2025, there are fewer than 75 human cases of H5N1 reported in the US. Getting to 10,000 cases by January 1, 2026, would require sustained, efficient human-to-human (H2H) transmission to begin almost immediately and result in an explosive, CDC-recognized outbreak. While a pandemic is theoretically possible, historical base rates of novel flu pandemics suggest the risk of one starting and reaching 10,000 confirmed cases within the next 7 months is exceptionally low (below 2%). The current risk assessment by the CDC remains 'low' with no evidence of H2H spread. Thus, this resolves YES only in the event of an imminent, rapid pandemic.

On his first day in office, President Trump issued an Executive Order setting a 90-day deadline (April 20, 2025) for the Secretaries of Defense and Homeland Security to recommend whether to invoke the Insurrection Act to address border control. Leading up to this deadline, major outlets like CNN and Politico reported that Secretaries Hegseth and Noem intended to advise against invoking the Act 'for now', citing currently low border crossings and a lack of need for additional authorities. The April 20 deadline passed without Trump invoking the Act. 

Although Trump's historical interest in the Insurrection Act and his formal request for a recommendation elevated the probability significantly earlier in the year, the immediate threat has subsided. His own loyalist cabinet members pushed back, and Trump has, historically (such as in 2020), backed down from actually invoking the Act when advised against it by top officials. However, there are still nearly 8 months remaining in 2025. A significant spike in border crossings or widespread domestic unrest could prompt him to reconsider this option. Consequently, the baseline risk is elevated compared to historical averages, but the failure to invoke it at the peak of the policy's momentum in April suggests it is not his immediate plan. Therefore, a probability of 16% balances his demonstrated interest against the recent negative recommendation from his advisors.

In February 2025, Interim DC U.S. Attorney Ed Martin initiated 'Operation Whirlwind', an investigation into Senator Chuck Schumer over 2020 remarks, asserting it was about threats against DOGE employees. This widely covered incident strongly pushed toward a YES resolution. However, by early March, mainstream reporting confirmed that DOJ leadership (Main Justice) effectively blocked Martin from elevating the probe to a grand jury, leading him to drop 'Operation Whirlwind'. This reveals significant institutional resistance within the DOJ against prosecuting high-profile Democrats based on these theories, even under the Trump administration. The community prediction on May 1, 2025, stood at 0.50, strongly implying that the Schumer probe did not clearly satisfy the strict criteria for a 'DOJ announcement of a new investigation' in the minds of the admins or the forecasting community given the DOJ’s internal squashing of the effort, or at least that it remains hotly debated. Nevertheless, the fact that an attempt was made so early in the term (February) underscores the severe political pressure on the DOJ to pursue Trump's political opponents. With almost 8 months left until the 2026 deadline, Trump could replace defiant DOJ officials or appoint a Special Counsel who will carry out these promises. Given the ongoing tension between Trump’s retribution promises and the demonstrated institutional guardrails at Main Justice, the probability hovers around a coin flip.

The PP currently leads the PSOE by a margin of 6 points (34% to 28%) in the Politico Poll of Polls average. The PP has consistently led in private polls and won both the July 2023 general election and the June 2024 European elections. While the state-run CIS poll historically favors the PSOE, Politico's aggregate methodology incorporates numerous private pollsters that universally show a solid PP lead (e.g., Sigma Dos gives PP a massive 152-seat projection). On 2025-05-18, the FIDE list will be identical to the May 1 list (published May 1, updated on Wikipedia May 2) where Humpy is ranked #5. This exactly matches her ranking on the cutoff date of 2025-05-11, so it resolves YES almost certainly (probability 0.95, accounting for Wikipedia vandalism or early updates). 

For the June 10, August 9, and November 7 dates, her ranking depends on live classical chess events. She is currently #5 at 2543 Elo. Aleksandra Goryachkina is at 2544 (#4), Zhu Jiner (#6 at 2541), and Tan Zhongyi (#7 at 2536). The Grosslobming FIDE Women's Grand Prix is running from May 6-15, 2025. Humpy is not competing. Tan Zhongyi scored 2/2 in the first two rounds, which should boost her live rating by around 10 Elo, pushing her above 2543 to surpass Humpy. Zhu Jiner has also racked up 3 straight wins recently. Because multiple top women are active while Humpy's rating remains stagnant, it is very likely one of them (especially Tan Zhongyi or Zhu Jiner) will overtake her 2543 rating by the June 1 list. Thus, her ranking will likely drop to #6 or lower, making it lower than her #5 ranking on May 11. Probabilities trail downwards to reflect this expectation and longer time horizons.

A vaccine for BK virus does not currently exist, and there are no late-stage clinical trials for one (ongoing trials focus on monoclonal antibodies and T-cell therapies, not true vaccines). Given the typical timeline for vaccine development, approval, and subsequent updating of Wikipedia articles, the probability of a vaccine being developed, recognized, and added to the 'List of infectious diseases' Wikipedia page in the next few months is near zero. The probabilities slightly increase for later dates to account for general uncertainty over longer time horizons.

The resolution depends on Aleksandra Goryachkina maintaining a FIDE rank of 4 or higher (i.e. rank 1, 2, 3, or 4). The Wikipedia FIDE rankings page updates at the start of each month when FIDE releases its new rating lists. For the first resolution date (2025-05-18), the May 1st list will still be active, meaning her rank will remain exactly where it is today (Rank 4). Therefore, the probability for this first date is extremely high. 
For subsequent dates (June, August, November), new FIDE lists will apply. Goryachkina is currently at 2544, which is 8 points behind the #3 player (Lei Tingjie at 2552). However, she is being closely trailed by several active players — Koneru Humpy is just 1 point behind (2543) and Zhu Jiner is only 3 points behind (2541). Because she is much closer to dropping a rank than gaining one, a random walk in ratings suggests she is more likely than not to fall to 5th or lower in the coming months. Therefore, the probability decreases as the time horizon extends, drifting slightly below 50% for the later dates due to the asymmetric distance to adjacent ranks.

On May 11, Bibisara Assaubayeva's rating listed on Wikipedia is 2494. A 1% increase requires her rating to hit at least 2519 (2494 * 1.01 = 2518.94). FIDE ratings operate on a monthly release cycle, updating precisely on the 1st of every month, meaning Wikipedia's FIDE rankings for May 18 will identically reflect the May 1st rating of 2494. This yields a near-zero probability for resolving YES on May 18. By June, August, and November, new FIDE lists will be published. Recent news from mid-April 2025 states that she recently breached a live rating of 2509.3 and clinched her Grandmaster title. Given she is active (e.g., competing in the Women's Grand Prix and the Grenke festival in April), she has ample opportunities to gain the remaining ~10 rating points required to cross 2519. Doing so quickly by June is plausible but challenging; however, by November, a rising 21-year-old talent has a very realistic chance of accumulating the points needed. Therefore, uncertainty and probability increase significantly across the forecasted timeline.

Adam Peaty has held the men's 50m breaststroke long course world record (25.95) since July 2017, and the record has survived numerous World Championships. The closest active competitor is Qin Haiyang, whose personal best is 26.20—a massive 0.25 seconds away from Peaty's mark, which is a very significant gap in a 50m sprint. Since there are no major international long-course championships set before mid-June, the record is nearly certain to survive the first two resolution dates (limited only by the small chance of an unannounced record at a national trial). The main test for the record will be the 2025 World Aquatics Championships in Singapore, running from July 11 to August 3, 2025. While there is a non-trivial chance that Qin Haiyang or another swimmer could drop a historic swim there, the 8-year durability of this record and the sheer size of the gap to the rest of the field make it highly probable that Peaty will retain the record through the summer and the rest of 2025. Even if Peaty breaks his own record, the question resolves YES because he would still hold it. Thus, probabilities remain quite high, slowly decaying after the World Championships.

The Wikipedia table currently has a blank field for a Botulism vaccine. The recent literature (as of early 2025) confirms there are no FDA-approved vaccines for botulism, with some candidates still in development or early testing phases. Developing, testing, and approving a vaccine typically takes years, and botulism is not currently an acute global pandemic that would see a vaccine fast-tracked in the span of 1 to 6 months. The only real pathway to a "Yes" resolution would be an editor erroneously adding a candidate or historically discontinued IND vaccine to the Wikipedia page and that edit surviving until the resolution date. Given the high profile of medical pages on Wikipedia and the speed at which incorrect edits are typically reverted, this is highly unlikely. The probabilities start at the minimum allowed limit (0.05) and increase slightly over longer horizons to account for unexpected developments or sustained Wikipedia edits.

Venezuelan hemorrhagic fever (caused by the Guanarito virus) currently has no vaccine. A web search confirmed that there are no late-stage clinical trials or imminent approvals for a vaccine against this pathogen; research is mostly limited to basic virology and early experimental models (such as the 2024 reverse genetics study). Given the decades-long typical timeline for vaccine development for neglected tropical diseases, the likelihood of a vaccine being successfully developed, approved, and officially recorded on the Wikipedia 'List of infectious diseases' page within the 6-month forecast horizon is negligible. Probabilities are set strictly near the lowest allowed boundary, increasing slightly across the forecast horizon to account for temporal uncertainty.

The question resolves based on the Wikipedia article 'List of infectious diseases' and whether the vaccine column for 'Hemorrhagic fever with renal syndrome (HFRS)' is populated (i.e., 'Yes' vs 'No' / empty). Currently (as of late April 2025), this column is empty, which resolves to 'No'. However, in the real world, an inactivated hantavirus vaccine (e.g., Hantavax) used against HFRS does exist and is administered in countries like South Korea and China. Because the vaccine factually exists, any Wikipedia editor (or even a forecaster participating in this market) could validly add this information to the article, and it would likely be retained as a factual, good-faith contribution. The probability of this happening naturally or via forecaster intervention starts low in the near term (7 days) but accumulates over the 6-month horizon. I have structured the probabilities to increase steadily over time, approaching 0.50 for the most distant date as uncertainty regarding whether the table will be updated grows.

The question asks whether Lei Tingjie will maintain or improve her current ranking position on FIDE's Women's list on four future dates. Her ranking on 2025-05-11 (based on the May 1, 2025 Wikipedia update) is #3, with a rating of 2552. To resolve YES, she must be ranked #1, #2, or #3 on the resolution dates. She holds an 8-point lead over #4 (Aleksandra Goryachkina, 2544) and a 9-point lead over #5 (Koneru Humpy, 2543). Lei recently withdrew from the 2024-25 FIDE Women's Grand Prix for personal reasons, making her somewhat inactive and freezing her classical rating in the immediate term. To overtake her, her competitors must achieve significant plus-scores in upcoming tournaments (like the final WGP events). 
1. **2025-05-18**: The May FIDE list will still be active. Wikipedia relies entirely on the FIDE publication which only updates on the 1st of the month. Thus, her ranking is practically guaranteed to still be #3 (0.95). 
2. **2025-06-10**: Based on the June list. Unless Goryachkina or another close competitor goes on a massive tear in May events (+9 rating points in high-level round robins is difficult though possible), Lei will likely maintain #3 (0.75).
3. **2025-08-09**: By August, more variance applies, and the FIDE Women's World Cup generally takes place in the summer. Rating stagnation makes her a target, and active players occasionally bleed points during knockouts (0.58).
4. **2025-11-07**: Over 6 months out, standard rating variance and the completion of major events like the Grand Swiss and World Cup make it nearly a coin-toss whether she holds #3, as others could easily hit 2553+ or she could return and drop points (0.48). This perfectly aligns with the requirement that uncertainty increases with the forecast horizon.

Actinomycosis is a bacterial disease treated with antibiotics (penicillin). A vaccine does not currently exist, and there are no clinical trials indicating one will exist anytime soon. Therefore, the chance of a vaccine being created and listed on Wikipedia by any of the 2025 resolution dates is negligible. The slight increase in probability over time reflects general uncertainty over longer horizons (and possible Wikipedia vandalism), but the underlying base rate of novel vaccine development within 6 months is functionally zero.

For Alexandra Kosteniuk to have a rating 1% higher than in early May (around 2479-2482), she would need to reach ~2504-2507, requiring an increase of approximately +25 Elo points. 

1. May 18: FIDE ratings and the Wikipedia FIDE pages update monthly (the newest list having just been finalized on May 1). The value will therefore be identical to May 11, meaning a 0% change. Thus, the probability for May 18 is effectively 0 (submitted as 0.02).
2. June 10: This will reflect her performance in May, primarily the ongoing Women's Grand Prix in Austria. Because her K-factor is 10, gaining 25 points in one 11-round tournament requires outperforming her expected score by ~2.5 to 3 full points. This is an extraordinarily dominant performance. Since she already seems to have dropped Game 1 to a lower-rated player (Olga Badelka), the odds of an immediate huge leap are extremely minimal. 
3. Aug 9 & Nov 7: As the time horizon expands over several months, the variance in Elo scores naturally compounds. A player engaging in 20-30 matches over 6 months has an Elo standard deviation between 19 and 27 points (Formula: sqrt(N * K^2 * p * (1-p))). While she's slowly declined in rating recently vs her peak (>2550), it's far from impossible for her to string together a strong Olympiad or string of tournaments that vaults her back above 2505. Hence, probability steadily trends upward toward 25% by the end of the year.

The baseline rating on the Wikipedia page on 2025-05-11 is 2751. A 1% increase requires Wesley So's rating to reach at least 2778.51, which effectively means achieving a rating of 2779. FIDE ratings are only updated on the 1st of each month. Therefore, on 2025-05-18, the official rating listed on the Wikipedia page will remain exactly 2751, making a 1% increase virtually impossible (0% chance under normal circumstances, factoring in slight risks of page vandalism or unexpected mid-month updates gives 2%).   For subsequent dates (June, August, November 2025), So would need to actively gain 28 rating points. In the modern chess elite at the 2750+ level, gaining nearly 30 points is notoriously difficult and requires dominant performances (typically +5 or higher) in super-tournaments. Over recent years, Wesley So's classical playstyle has been noted for its extraordinary solidity and high draw rate, rarely resulting in drastic rating swings in either direction. While a steady climb is possible over the long term, doing so within 1-6 months is highly improbable. As the forecast horizon extends to November, the variance increases, leading to a slightly higher estimate.

Metagonimiasis is an infectious disease caused by an intestinal parasitic fluke. It is easily treated with Praziquantel, prevented by avoiding undercooked freshwater fish, and has seen declining human incidence due to improved sanitation and public health measures. No vaccine currently exists, nor is any in development. Developing, testing, and approving a vaccine takes many years, and there is no financial or public health incentive to create one for this specific, easily treatable parasite. The only possible way this question could resolve 'Yes' is if the Wikipedia page 'List of infectious diseases' is vandalized with false information that remains uncorrected precisely on the resolution dates. This is highly unlikely. Therefore, the probability remains extremely low close to the floor (0.02). Slight increases over time (0.02 to 0.04) are included strictly to account for the mechanically increasing uncertainty over longer forecast horizons, per instructions.

For the first resolution date (May 18, 2025), FIDE ratings are updated monthly (on the 1st), so the Wikipedia list on May 18 will be identical to May 11, making an increase >1% effectively impossible. A 1% increase requires her to gain ~25 Elo points. For the June 10 date, achieving a 25-point gain in just the month of May requires a highly notable tournament performance, which is rare for players at the 2500 level. As the horizon extends to August and November, she will have more tournament opportunities to accumulate rating points. Since her peak rating previously exceeded 2530, a 25+ point return to 2519+ over a 6-month period is highly plausible, though not the baseline expectation, so probabilities rise correspondingly to reflect increasing uncertainty.

There is currently no vaccine for Chlamydophila pneumoniae, and recent research (2023) confirms that candidates are only in the early pre-clinical or in-silico design stages. Moreover, historical trials for this pathogen have failed. Developing a vaccine from in-silico to clinical availability takes roughly 5 to 10+ years. Therefore, it is practically impossible that a vaccine will be developed and appropriately cited on the Wikipedia 'List of infectious diseases' page by any of the 2025 resolution dates. My probabilities are at or near the 0.05 minimum boundary permitted by the instructions, with a tiny upward drift over time to account for long-tail risks, such as vandalism or a user prematurely adding an experimental vaccine to the list.

Rickettsialpox is a rare and typically mild, self-limiting disease caused by the bacterium Rickettsia akari. It is easily and effectively treated with antibiotics such as doxycycline. Because of its rarity and the efficacy of current treatments, there is no pressing commercial or public health incentive to develop a vaccine for it, and there are currently no known development efforts or clinical trials underway for a Rickettsialpox vaccine. The timeline to develop, test, and approve a vaccine is years to decades, making it virtually impossible for one to be developed between May 2025 and November 2025. The only pathway for this question to resolve as 'Yes' would be through temporary Wikipedia vandalism or an erroneous edit that remains on the resolution date. This is highly unlikely on an active page. Therefore, the baseline probability is at the floor (0.02), increasing slightly over the horizon as a hedge against unexpected Wikipedia artifacts.

The Wikipedia page for 'List of infectious diseases' currently does not list a vaccine for Coccidioidomycosis (Valley Fever), counting as a 'No'. A human vaccine is in early stages of development (recent NIH funding to adapt a canine candidate) and is years away from approval. A canine vaccine (Deltacps1 by Anivive) is in development and expected to seek approval 'by the end of 2025'. Since the canine vaccine is not likely to be approved before late 2025, and it is uncertain whether a veterinary vaccine would be added to a Wikipedia list primarily focused on human infectious diseases, the probability of the Wikipedia page being updated to reflect a vaccine by the resolution dates is very low. Resolving dates cover May through November 2025, with probability staying near the minimum bound given the extensive timeline required for vaccine approval and Wikipedia updating.

The Wikipedia article 'List of infectious diseases' currently does not list any developed vaccine for Clonorchiasis, leaving the column blank. This corresponds to the known 'No' value as of May 1, 2025. Web searches confirm that while there is ongoing research into recombinant and oral vaccine candidates against Clonorchis sinensis, these have only been tested in animal models (e.g., mice and rats). There are no human clinical trials for a Clonorchiasis vaccine currently underway, let alone any that are on the verge of regulatory approval. Due to the multi-year timeframe required to advance a vaccine from preclinical animal models to human approval, the probability of a vaccine being successfully developed and officially listed on the Wikipedia page by late 2025 is exceedingly low. The only plausible scenario for a 'Yes' resolution would be temporary vandalism or an erroneous edit on Wikipedia on exactly the respective resolution dates. In accordance with the instruction to increase uncertainty over time, the probability begins at the minimum allowed (0.02) and marginally grows to 0.04 for the final date.

The question asks if Hikaru Nakamura will maintain a FIDE ranking of 1 or 2 on four distinct resolution dates. The FIDE rankings update on the 1st of every month. On the May 1, 2025 list, Nakamura is ranked #2 with a classical rating of 2804, safely 17 points ahead of #3 Gukesh (2787). For the May 18 resolution, the active list will be the May 1 list, locking the result at Rank 2. For June 10, the active list will be the June 1 list. Gukesh, Caruana, and others are playing in the Superbet Classic in May, but gaining 17+ Elo in a single super-GM classical event requires an almost physically impossible +4 or +5 score above expected, meaning Nakamura is highly protected through early June. For August 9, Norway Chess (held late May/early June) will be rated. The field is notoriously difficult (Carlsen, Caruana, Gukesh, Arjun), and a slight negative performance by Nakamura could shed 10-15 rating points, making it significantly easier for the active young prodigies to overtake him. Finally, by the November 7 resolution, a host of large events (including the FIDE World Cup) will have taken place. Given Nakamura's limited classical schedule and the immense upward trajectory of active players like Gukesh and Arjun, his chances of holding Rank #2 shrink considerably as the horizon lengthens.

The resolution requires Gukesh's Wikipedia-listed Elo rating to increase by at least 1% relative to its value on May 11, 2025. Per the May 11 Wikipedia page, his rating is 2787. A 1% increase equates to gaining roughly 28 Elo points, requiring him to reach at least 2815. 

Gaining 28 points at the 2780+ level requires an extended winning streak with performance ratings exceeding 2900, an exceptional feat accomplished by very few players in history over short timeframes. 

1. For May 18: FIDE ratings update monthly (on the 1st of the month), and the Wikipedia page mirrors these official lists. The May 18th page will reflect the same May 1st list as May 11th, so his rating will remain exactly 2787. (Probability = 0.02)
2. For June 10: The metric will rely on the June 1 list. Gukesh does not have enough high-level classical games finishing before June 1 (Norway chess extends into early June) to generate a 28-point shift. (Probability = 0.03)
3. For Aug 9 & Nov 7: Reflecting the August and November lists, more super-tournaments like Norway Chess and Grand Chess Tour events will be rated. While Gukesh is elite and improving, breaching 2815 is a monumental milestone (would make him top-10 historic peaks). The probability climbs slightly due to the extended timeframe and more opportunities, but remains very low. (Probability = 0.05, 0.08)

The resolution requires Gukesh's Wikipedia-listed Elo rating to increase by at least 1% relative to its value on May 11, 2025. Per the May 11 Wikipedia page, his rating is 2787. A 1% increase equates to gaining roughly 28 Elo points, requiring him to reach at least 2815.

The Wikipedia page "List of infectious diseases" currently leaves the "Vaccine(s)" cell blank for Epidemic typhus, which evaluates to a "No" status. There is no commercially available vaccine for Epidemic typhus at present, and the disease is easily treated with antibiotics, so there is little ongoing development for a new modern clinical vaccine (most recent literature is for scrub typhus or typhoid fever, which are different diseases). A historical vaccine (Weigl's vaccine) did exist in the 1930s, but since it is no longer used, Wikipedia editors have left the column blank. The probability of an editor deciding to add it to this specific table in the next 1-6 months is very small, hence the consistently low probabilities.

Isosporiasis is a neglected protozoan infection (caused by Cystoisospora belli) primarily affecting immunocompromised patients. It is treated with anti-protozoal medications like TMP-SMX. There is currently no vaccine available or in late-stage clinical trials, and virtually zero commercial incentive to develop one. Developing a human or animal vaccine for a protozoan parasite typically takes decades. The Wikipedia 'List of infectious diseases' currently shows a blank cell for the Isosporiasis vaccine, which the resolution criteria explicitly map to 'No'. The likelihood of a real vaccine being developed, approved, and added to this Wikipedia page within the next 6 months is negligible. The only real avenue for a 'Yes' resolution would be an erroneous Wikipedia edit (vandalism) that remains uncorrected on the resolution dates. Giving the minimum allowed 0.05 probability for the early dates, increasing slightly to 0.08 for the furthest date to reflect increasing uncertainty.

The yfinance tool provides a combined probability estimate of 0.41 for the 1-week horizon based on AbbVie's (ABBV) recent downward momentum, as the stock fell from over $191 to $180.26 in early May. In accordance with the instruction that weekly stock moves are largely random and predictions should stay close to 0.5 without a very strong signal, we peg the 1-week forecast at 0.45. For the longer-term resolution dates (1-month, 3-months, 6-months), the initial momentum signal decays. Adhering to the instruction that uncertainty should increase with the forecast horizon and probabilities should move closer to 0.5, the estimates steadily revert to a near-neutral random-walk expectation, ending at a slightly positive 0.51 at 6 months to account for the intrinsic positive drift of large equity stocks over longer periods.

The baseline close price for the metric is $21.21 (from Friday, May 9, taking the place of Sunday, May 11).

Short-term (May 18, June 10): The stock faces near-term negative momentum. Despite Amentum strongly beating Q2 EPS estimates ($0.53 vs $0.26 expected) on May 7, it exhibited a "sell-the-news" reaction, dropping from $22.11 to $21.21 over three days. Crucially, Jacobs Solutions is carrying out a special dividend that will distribute 7.3 million AMTM shares to its shareholders. The record date is May 16, with physical distribution right around May 30. This influx of shares (approx. 3% of shares outstanding) into the hands of potentially uninterested Jacobs shareholders creates a significant, quantifiable supply overhang that will exert selling pressure throughout late May and early June. Consequently, probabilities for the first two dates remain tilted downward.

Long-term (August 9, November 7): Amentum sits at a strong fundamental valuation. An annualized EPS approaching $2.00+ on a $21 stock implies a forward P/E of roughly 10x. Additionally, their recent $360M divestiture of a low-growth unit to Lockheed Martin will accelerate necessary debt deleveraging, strengthening the balance sheet. Once the market absorbs the special dividend's mechanical selling pressure, this underlying value and standard long-term equity growth should buoy the stock. Therefore, probabilities tilt back slightly positive but revert closely to 0.5, obeying the heuristic for increasing forecast horizon uncertainty.

The YFinance statistical tool predicts a probability of 0.41 for exceeding the current threshold (353.58) over the next week, driven by a recent negative trend (-0.62 per day) and a post-earnings miss slump from April 25. Thus, the near-term forecast for May 18 is anchored slightly below 0.5 at 0.43. As the forecast horizon increases to June, August, and November, stock prices are dominated by random walk dynamics and uncertainty increases. By following the instruction to move distant dates closer to 0.5, probabilities are gradually adjusted upward to 0.46, 0.48, and 0.50 respectively, reflecting typical mean-reversion and the long-term positive drift of equities.

The reference class is the probability of a randomly selected individual stock's price exceeding its current price after periods of 1 week, 1 month, 3 months, and 6 months. Historically, due to the equity risk premium, this drifts slightly above 50% (typically 51-54%). The fetch_ts_yfinance tool returned a statistical expectation of P=0.56. Following a recent positive Q1 earnings release on April 29, the stock rebounded significantly (from ~$63 to ~$67) with several analysts maintaining "Hold" or moving price targets slightly higher into the mid-to-high $70s. AOS has also reaffirmed a $400M solid buyback program for 2025, which provides a price floor, leading to positive near-term post-earnings announcement drift (PEAD). By contrast, quarterly cash dividends (~$0.34 each in late July/October) will mechanically drag down the price return over the 3-mon and 6-mon horizons. Adhering to the prompt's instruction to make uncertainty increase and thus move longer-dated forecasts closer to 0.5, I gradually pull back toward 0.5 for the later dates.

The base rate is established by the YFinance summary linear estimate: P(value > 156.4) = 0.52 for the shortest horizon (May 18). Since markets slightly drift upwards over time, P(higher) slightly increases. Earnings reported on May 7th were strong, beating EPS by a few cents ($3.03 vs. $2.90) and lifting EPS guidance. This triggered a short-term rise and the stock price has shown strong upward momentum. The ex-dividend date associated with the June 9 payout should occur around May 26. Since the May 18 date is before the ex-div drop (~$0.87), predicting the price to remain roughly equal to its May 9 close (156.38) yields P=0.54. Because ATO has strong fundamental earnings momentum, long-term trends push the probability slightly higher as time expands, climbing towards ~60% over 6 months because the market has mathematically positive expectancy of ~6-10% a year, overcoming occasional ~$0.87 quarterly dividend drops. Therefore the predicted probabilities progressively shift up: 0.54, 0.56, 0.58, 0.61.

The exact baseline price is the closing price on May 9, which was 143.33. Stock prices generally follow a random walk with a slight upward drift over time. For the short term, this baseline is heavily impacted by the upcoming ex-dividend date on May 13, where AWK will pay out a significantly increased dividend of $0.8275 per share. This mechanical price drop presents a major headwind for the May 18 resolution date, pulling the probability well below 0.5. As the forecast horizon extends to June and August, the natural positive drift has more time to compensate for this initial dividend drop (and a likely subsequent August dividend), pulling the probabilities closer to 0.5. By November, 6 months of expected drift slightly edges out the accumulated dividend drag, resulting in a probability just over 0.5. In combination with increasing time-horizon volatility that pushes distribution tails outward, this naturally converges the binary P(higher) closer to 0.5 for the most distant dates as uncertainty dominates the specific outcome.

CAT's closing price on the forecast date (effectively Friday, May 9, 2025) was 322.75. Predicting stock movements over specific weekly/monthly horizons is notoriously difficult and heavily mean-reverting. 

In the short term (1 week to 1 month), CAT has strong positive momentum. The stock crashed to $282 in mid-April due to the announcement of new 'Trump tariffs' and general industrial selloffs, but has since embarked on a V-shaped relief rally, recovering to $322.75. This short-term bullish momentum slightly favors the stock staying above the threshold price, justifying a probability slightly above 0.5 for the May and June resolution dates.

In the medium to long term (3 to 6 months), major headwinds remain. Management has explicitly warned of a $250 million to $350 million negative impact from tariffs in Q2 2025 and projected flat-to-declining 2025 sales overall. Q2 earnings in late July are likely to highlight these fundamental impacts, which will counteract the typical upward 'drift' seen in large-cap equities. Additionally, the stock will go ex-dividend (typically $1.41) in July and October, slightly dragging down the share price relative to the fixed May 9 threshold. 

Following the strict guidance that weekly stock moves are largely unpredictable and that probabilities should approach 0.50 as the forecast horizon and uncertainty increase, I am using a conservative, smoothly decaying set of estimates: 0.53, 0.52, 0.51, and 0.50.

The baseline price for the question is 138.79 (the market close on May 9, 2025, which corresponds to the May 11 weekend resolution). CRL experienced a massive structural hit in early April when the FDA announced a phase-out of animal testing, causing shares to plunge from the $160s to the $100s. The recent surge to 138.79 on May 9 was driven by Q1 earnings, massive share buybacks, and the announcement of a strategic review and board shakeup. However, this short-squeeze/activist premium appears to have peaked on May 8th (hitting 141.36) before pulling back on May 9th. The fundamental impairment from the FDA ruling and declining organic revenue suggest this elevated price will act as a ceiling, though a strategic review could provide a floor. Given the linear model prediction of a 0.45 probability to exceed the threshold and fading momentum, I am assigning a slight edge to a pullback in the short term, with probabilities reverting to 0.5 for longer horizons as uncertainty grows.

In keeping with the principle that short-term predictable positive drift becomes overwhelmed by noise and dividend drags over time, our uncertainty increases and the probability decays toward exactly 0.50 for the most distant resolution dates.

The DXCM stock has just seen a massive positive catalyst with its May 1, 2025 earnings release, beating expectations with a strong showing and a new $750M share buyback, along with an FDA clearance for the G7 15-day system. The stock jumped from ~70 pre-earnings to 84.67 by May 9. Wall Street average target is $99. Due to post-earnings momentum and buyback support, the shortest horizon (May 18) has a good chance (>50%) of holding above or climbing slightly from 84.67. 
    However, stocks typically exhibit a random walk with slight positive drift on average, but after sharp ~20% rallies, mean reversion or profit-taking can slightly offset the momentum over medium-term horizons. Since the starting price is May 9 close (84.67) and the question asks if it will be higher than the May 11 price (which resolves to May 9 close since May 11 is Sunday), we are predicting if it stays above 84.67.
    The yfinance combined estimate for a short horizon gives 0.56 based on trend and seasonality (May typically seems decent for DXCM). I'll place the May 18 probability at 0.59 given the immediate momentum and buyback support, scaling down to 0.56, 0.54, and 0.51 for the later dates as the post-earnings momentum fades into pure random walk and general market noise.

The question asks whether ECL's price will be above 250.07 on four future dates (7 days, 1 month, 3 months, 6 months). Stock prices approximate a random walk with a modest positive drift over time. Ecolab recently had very strong earnings on April 29th, pushing the price up sharply from ~236 to ~252. There has been a mild pullback in the last week (to 250.07). Over the one week horizon, the momentum and random walk keep the probability near 50%, perhaps slightly below due to short-term pullback (forecast: 0.49). As the time horizon expands, the equity risk premium/market drift dictates that probability of a positive return steadily increases. The baseline probability of a positive return for a large cap stock is around 52-54% over 1 month, 55-56% over 3 months, and 57-60% over 6 months. Therefore, my estimates progressively increase towards 0.58.

The major near-term hurdle for ED stock against the unadjusted baseline of 104.20 is the impending ex-dividend date on May 14, 2025, which will knock roughly $0.85 off the price. Over the next week (by May 18), expected price return drift is negligible compared to this drop. Using an estimated annualized volatility of 20%, the stock has a ~38% chance of overcoming this dividend hurdle to jump back past 104.20 within just 5 days. For June 10, the stock has slightly more time (~1 month) for its ~4.5% expected annual price drift to help it recover, bringing the probability closer to 46%. For August 9, only one dividend drop will have been incurred, while 3 months of drift will have accumulated, creating a near-even neutral outcome around 51%. By November 7, a second dividend drop (Q3) will have hit, but 6 months of expected drift almost perfectly offsets the $1.70 in accumulated dividend payouts, keeping the long-term unadjusted odds balanced very close to 52%. Standard deviation dominates the effect of drift and dividends over long horizons, driving the estimates toward 0.50 as uncertainty increases.

However, due to recent rapid momentum exhaustion near $267.5, a moderate near-term consolidation is highly plausible. For the June 10 resolution, the stock will have gone ex-dividend (record date May 23, $0.50/share payout), providing a slight technical handicap, bringing the probability to 0.46. As the horizon expands to August 9 and November 7, random-walk dynamics dominate over near-term mean reversion, and general equity drift brings the probabilities closer to 0.5 in alignment with the directive that uncertainty increases over longer forecast horizons.

The fetch_ts_yfinance statistical model outputs a 0.55 probability of the stock increasing past its most recent May 9 close price. Following best practices for short-term asset price movements, which often resemble random walks, we use 0.55 as the anchor probability for the nearest resolution date. Our web searches regarding recent downgrades (e.g., Baird downgrading ELV from Outperform to Neutral with a lower price target) do not reflect a dramatic enough catalyst to override the statistical benchmark. Finally, adhering to the instruction that uncertainty should increase with longer forecast horizons, the probability of exceeding the initial price smoothly regresses toward 0.5 for the later resolution dates.

The threshold value EXPE must exceed on the resolution dates is $155.39 (its close price on May 9, 2025). On May 8, Expedia reported a significant net loss, a Q1 revenue miss, and downgraded its full-year guidance for 2025, sending the stock tumbling 8% to $155.39 on May 9. Analyst reactions have been negative, with Piper Sandler downgrading EXPE to Underweight with a $135 price target, and others lowering targets prior to earnings. Immediate post-earnings momentum combined with institutional downgrades points to a lack of near-term catalysts to snap back above the threshold level. According to statistical modeling, there is about a 40% chance the stock recovers above $155.39 by May 18. Due to lowered full-year guidance and broader macroeconomic softness in US travel demand, the stock seems moderately likely to underperform in the short- to medium-term. To reflect standard market drift and increasing uncertainty, probabilities revert toward 0.5 for longer horizons.

The FICO stock closed at 2088.22 on 2025-05-09. Over the month of April and early May 2025, FICO reported robust Q2 earnings on April 29, leading to an analyst target boost to $2,230 on May 1. The stock has therefore jumped from around 1816 to 2088, driven by strong fundamentals. FICO's guidance reiterates double-digit percentage growth for both revenue and earnings.
Given the general positive drift of the stock market and FICO's recent strong performance, the probability of the stock being higher on the resolution dates increases gradually as the time horizon grows.

For 2025-05-18 (very short horizon): The price change is likely to be small; there's a slight upside drift but also a chance of post-earnings profit-taking. Probability: 0.53.
For 2025-06-10 (1 month horizon): Trend tends to continue, slightly higher equity premium. Probability: 0.54.
For 2025-08-09 (3 months horizon): Greater potential for the upward structural drift to manifest. Probability: 0.55.
For 2025-11-07 (6 months horizon): Significant time for the equity risk premium and FICO's consistent earnings growth to lift the stock higher. Probability: 0.57.

The resolution criteria require predicting if GDDY's market close price will be strictly higher than its closing price on 2025-05-11. Since 2025-05-11 is a Sunday, the comparison price is the close from Friday, 2025-05-09, which is 182.60. 

Recent earnings saw GDDY beat EPS and revenue estimates, yet the stock sold off from around 192.36 down to 176.27 before recovering slightly to 182.60. Analysts maintain high price targets (most over $210 to $250), which gives a bullish long-term picture, but the immediate post-earnings momentum and statistical trend estimation are bearish. The Yahoo Finance series fetch tool returned a combined estimate of 0.41 for a near-term increase above the threshold, heavily weighting the short-term negative post-earnings response and seasonal behavior.

As instructed, I am treating the immediate timeframe as largely unpredictable but anchoring on the tools' 0.41 combined statistical estimate, adjusting it slightly to 0.43 to reflect the high analyst price targets. Following the strict instruction that uncertainty must increase over long time horizons (meaning probabilities for more distant dates must progressively regress toward 0.5), I project the probability to drift upward over the further dates.

The resolution criteria require predicting if GDDY's market close price will be strictly higher than its closing price on 2025-05-11. Since 2025-05-11 is a Sunday, the comparison price is the close from Friday, 2025-05-09, which is 182.60. 

Recent earnings saw GDDY beat EPS and revenue estimates, yet the stock sold off from around 192.36 down to 176.27 before recovering slightly to 182.60. Analysts maintain high price targets (most over $210 to $250), which gives a bullish long-term picture, but the immediate post-earnings momentum and statistical trend estimation are bearish. The Yahoo Finance series fetch tool returned a combined estimate of 0.41 for a near-term increase above the threshold, heavily weighting the short-term negative post-earnings response and seasonal behavior.

The stock currently trades around $113.66 on the forecast date (May 11, 2025). The baseline statistical analysis via historical prices indicates short-term probabilities near 50%, with long-term upward drift typically pushing longer-horizon predictions higher. However, GPC declared a $1.03 quarterly dividend (approx. 0.9% of the share price) with an ex-dividend date of June 6, 2025. This will result in an automatic mechanical drop in the share price right before the June 10 resolution date, thereby providing a headwind that pushes the June 10 probability slightly below 0.5. For the August and November resolution dates, the longer-term market drift (assumed 8-10% annualized) is expected to offset the dividend-induced drops (including another likely dividend in early September), pushing the likelihood of being above the May 11 price slightly higher, into the low-to-mid 50s. Adhering to the guideline that remote horizon uncertainties pull probabilities close to 0.5, all estimates are placed very near 0.5.

HD's short-term momentum has been mildly negative, dropping from $400 in early Feb 2025 to ~$354 by early May. The yfinance statistical trend analysis model estimates approximately a 45% chance of the stock being higher by the 1-week mark (May 18). Thus, I set the Date 1 estimate slightly below 50% at 46%. Q1 earnings on May 20 will introduce volatility for Date 2 (1 month horizon); here, positive equity drift and potential earnings surprises begin to balance out the recent downtrend, leading to an estimate of 48%. For longer horizons (Dates 3 and 4 at 3 and 6 months out), the historical equity risk premium and typical positive drift of large-cap stocks start to dominate short-term noise and current momentum factors. This pushes the probabilities gradually over the 50% mark to 51% and 53%, respectively. This schedule stays close to 0.5 overall, reflecting the near-unpredictable random walk nature of stocks while accounting for initial negative momentum that reverts towards positive drift over extended periods.

The threshold for this question is HII's market close price for the baseline date of May 11, 2025, which corresponds to the last trading day's close on Friday, May 9 (approximately $227.39). HII has seen a strong stock price rally over the past two months (rising from the $170 range in March). Recent developments include its Q1 2025 earnings report on May 1, where the company reaffirmed its annual guidance, prompting several major analysts (including those from Barclays and Goldman Sachs) to raise or reiterate their price targets (averaging around $233.61–$243.61 for the year ahead). This analyst consensus is above the threshold price, which supports near-to-medium-term positive bias.

Recently, the stock has consolidated its gains, trading consistently between $225 and $229 over the past week or so. Although there was a small amount of insider selling recorded on May 6, the fundamentals post-earnings remain intact. Yfinance's statistical model indicates roughly a 54%–60% probability of being above the threshold in the short window. Consistent with the prompt instructions that "uncertainty should INCREASE with forecast horizon," the probabilities are set favorably above the 0.5 coin-flip mark due to recent strong momentum, solid earnings, and raised price targets, but strict adherence to increasing statistical noise tapers the prediction down closer to 0.50 as the resolution dates go further out into the future.

The yFinance statistical model provided a combined short-term estimate of 0.39 based on a linear downward trend structure since February and negative historical seasonality in May. However, recent fundamental news (strong Q1 2025 earnings and $927M in capital returns to shareholders) triggered an 18% bounce from an April low of $204 to a current price of $242.48. Following such a rapid short-term spike, a slight pullback or period of consolidation is technically common, which supports keeping the short-term (May 18, June 10) probabilities below 0.5, honoring the statistical guidance. For the longer-term horizons (August 9, November 7), the inherent positive drift of the stock market, as well as HLT's aggressive share repurchases, naturally pull the expected probability upward. Per the instructions, uncertainty smoothly increases with the horizon, bringing the distant probabilities progressively closer to 0.50 and slightly above it for November, capturing the long-term upward drift.

The reference price on May 11 (using the May 9 close) is 56.75. HOLX had been in a severe downtrend since early 2025 due to China tariffs and lowered FY2025 revenue guidance, dropping from the low 70s to the 57-58 range in April. Following Q2 earnings on May 1, the price briefly plunged further to 52.73 before staging a sharp 4-day bounce to 56.75. This recent price action demonstrates strong short-term upward momentum, putting the stock right on the edge of its stable pre-earnings April trading range (~57-58). The time series analysis combining linear and seasonal trends provides a 56% baseline probability of being above the threshold in the short term. Per the prompt's explicit instructions, my uncertainty increases (probabilities converge toward 0.5) as the forecast horizon extends out to 6 months.

I modeled this by balancing the expected equity market drift against the substantial mechanical drag of Hershey's upcoming cash dividends (which affect the unadjusted closing price that Yahoo Finance uses for the resolution).
1. **Dividend Drag Mechanics:** The baseline price is the Friday, May 9 close ($165.80). Per Hershey’s Q2 dividend declaration, the record date is May 16. Under the new T+1 settlement rules, the ex-dividend date is now the same as the record date. Therefore, on Friday, May 16, the stock will open ~$1.37 lower (a ~0.8% drop). Since May 18 is a Sunday, its resolution resolves to the May 16 close. Asking the stock to completely earn back that 0.8% deficit over just 5 trading days imposes a heavy penalty, dropping the P(YES) below 50%. Assuming a random walk with historical volatility (around 3% weekly), the probability of exceeding the May 9 close falls to about 39%.
2. **Horizon Drift vs Dividends:**
- **June 10 (~1 month):** Only the May dividend ($1.37) has been paid. Expected price drift over a month helps mitigate the drop, raising the probability closer to 46%.
- **August 9 (~3 months):** Historically, Hershey's Q3 dividend ex-dates fall in mid-August (around August 16th). Thus, on August 8 (the close for the August 9 resolution), the stock will still only have absorbed the single May dividend. Three months of built-in equity drift mathematically neutralize the $1.37 headwind, returning the chance of being higher to basically 50/50.
- **November 7 (~6 months):** The stock will have absorbed the August dividend too (cumulative -$2.74 drag), but 6 months of expected drift offers a slight net positive bias, making a YES outcome marginally more likely than NO. However, soaring cocoa prices and highly volatile margins keep confidence low at long horizons, placing estimates very close to 0.52.

However, per the instructions, stock moves are largely unpredictable over short intervals so we stay close to 0.5. As the forecast horizon increases, broader market uncertainties dominate and the probability should be drawn even closer to 0.5, reflecting increased uncertainty over longer horizons.

1. **Short-Term Headwind (May 18):** Research confirmed that Kroger's next ex-dividend date is May 15, 2025, for $0.32 per share. Given that a week of standard positive market drift is much smaller than this ~0.45% instant price reduction, the probability of the stock closing higher on May 18 than on May 11 is structurally below 50%. Thus, the first estimate is modeled at 0.46.
2. **Medium-Term Support (June 10):** Following the collapse of the Albertsons merger, Kroger announced a massive $7.5 billion stock buyback program (representing ~15% of its market cap). This introduces strong, persistent buying pressure which should overcome the initial $0.32 drop within a month. Positive equities drift pulls the 30-day forecast slightly above 50% (estimated at 0.53).
3. **Long-Term Uncertainty (Aug 9, Nov 7):** As the horizon extends, fundamental uncertainties (upcoming earnings, impact of union opposition to buybacks, general macroeconomic conditions, and future quarterly dividend hits) increase. In strict adherence to the instructions to linearly increase uncertainty and pull distant probability dates closer to 0.5, the forecasts for August and November gradually converge toward 0.50 (0.52 and 0.51, respectively), satisfying the exact condition that the distance from 0.5 monotonically decreases with horizon over time.

1. Base rate & Initial Anchor: The `fetch_ts_yfinance` tool provides a combined statistical estimate of 0.60 for the stock being above the current dropped price (729.17) in the near term. This reflects potential mean reversion (bounce effect) plus seasonal trends following a massive 18% selloff over a 9-day period.

2. Fundamental drivers: The recent sharp drop was driven by two main factors—lowered EPS guidance (largely due to an acquisition/R&D charge) and CVS Caremark removing Zepbound from some standard formularies (favoring Novo's Wegovy). While these are substantive headwinds, 729.17 represents a severe oversold level near 1-year lows, making a short-term stabilization or rebound highly probable in the immediate term.

3. Time Horizon adjustment: According to the specific forecasting instructions, uncertainty must increase with the forecast horizon, pulling distant date probabilities closer to 0.5 (maximum uncertainty). As the horizon extends from 1 week (May 18) to 1 month (June 10), 3 months (August 9), and 6 months (November 7), the short-term mean-reversion signal decays into a random walk. Over the longer term, the fundamental effects of the CVS decision and competitive pricing pressures on weight-loss drugs present stronger two-sided risks.

4. Therefore, I anchor the 1-week forecast at the tool's 0.60 recommendation, and systematically fade the probabilities toward 0.50 for the longer-term resolution dates.

Based on the yfinance historical data, the stock has experienced a recent short-term negative linear trend (-0.02592 per day) over the past three months, producing a statistical probability of 0.47 of exceeding the current price threshold in the short term. The combined estimate, factoring in bearish seasonal patterns from last year, is 0.40. I am anchoring the short-term forecast (May 18) at 0.44 to reflect standard stock market unpredictability offset slightly by the short-term negative drift and seasonal effects. For dates further out in the horizon (June, August, and November), I am gradually regressing the probability toward 0.5. Although dividend ex-dates in July and October will marginally lower the unadjusted closing price, creating a small headwind, baseline expected equity drift over a multi-month period partially offsets this, keeping long-term probabilities near completely uncertain (0.50).

The stock recently rallied from $123.4 on April 21 to $136.52 on May 9 following a very strong Q1 2025 earnings report (massive net income surge and record deferred event-related revenue). However, $136.52 represents a local peak, and the statistical trend analysis tool estimates only a 0.40 probability for the stock to remain above this threshold in the short term, citing recent downward slope since its February highs and seasonal headwinds (0% of recent prior years showed an upward move during this specific seasonal window). Furthermore, the overarching DOJ antitrust lawsuit survived a motion to dismiss in early 2025, creating persistent legal overhang that might temper further immediate upside. Following the instructions, I have anchored my initial probability near the statistical estimate of 0.40 (adjusted slightly toward 0.50 due to the unpredictability of short-term stock moves and the strong Q1 fundamentals), and then trended the probability back toward 0.50 as the forecast horizon increases to represent growing uncertainty over longer time periods.
